topic: Two roommates disagree about getting a pet
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sheer size of the enclosure made my palms slick with sweat. Leo paced the living room, his voice tight with insistence. "It's just a small hamster, Maya. It won't cause any trouble." I kept my gaze fixed on the closed door of the spare room, my breath catching in shallow gasps. Every time he moved too quickly, a tremor ran through my legs, making me want to freeze solid to the spot. The thought of something small, living, and utterly dependent on me was a heavy weight pressing down on my chest. "But what if it gets out?" I managed to squeak out, my voice thin and reedy. I hugged my arms around myself, a futile attempt to shield my core. The silence stretched, thick and suffocating, punctuated only by the frantic thump of my own heart against my ribs.

[story 2]

I watched Leo carefully as he presented the adoption papers, his enthusiasm practically vibrating off him. He spoke about the creature with such open affection, his eyes shining with a kind of boundless joy. My stomach twisted into a tight knot. I started meticulously cleaning the already spotless kitchen counter, scrubbing at imaginary grime with unnecessary vigor. When he finally looked at me, his expression shifted, a flicker of something that mirrored my own internal turmoil. "Come on, Maya. Think about it. It would be wonderful." I didn't look up. My hands trembled slightly as I gripped the sponge, the cheap plastic feeling suddenly slick and dangerous. The air in the apartment seemed too thick to draw in, and I felt a distinct need to bolt, to disappear into the shadows.

[story 3]

The cage sat in the corner of the room, a small, furry anomaly amidst the organized chaos of our shared space. I kept glancing at it, a constant, unwanted focus. When Leo suggested we get one, a cold wave washed over me, making the fine hairs on my arms stand up. I found myself retreating to my room, pulling the curtains shut tight, creating a dim, cave-like space. Every time I heard Leo’s cheerful planning, a sharp, unpleasant tension coiled in my gut. I started pacing the length of my bedroom floor, my movements jerky and restless. I squeezed my eyes shut, trying to force the churning sensation in my belly to subside, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.
